title: Usability of large language models for building construction safety risk assessment
contribution: ChatGPT在建筑施工安全风险评估中提供了与专家相似的答案，在风险评分和预防措施选择上表现出色
abstract: This study evaluates the usability of ChatGPT as a decision-support tool for occupational health and safety risk assessment in building construction by comparing its responses with those of ten human experts across 71 risks in 12 work items. Using a Modified Manhattan Distance similarity metric and statistical testing, the results show no significant difference between expert and ChatGPT risk scores, with ChatGPT demonstrating higher inter-session consistency than human experts.
methodology: Ten experts and ten independent ChatGPT sessions rated 71 risks across 12 building construction work items using a 5-point Likert scale covering probability and multiple severity dimensions, with response similarity quantified via a Modified Manhattan Distance metric and risk score differences tested using two-sample Student's t-tests.
results: No statistically significant difference was found between expert and ChatGPT risk scores under both TRS (p=0.54) and TotRS (p=0.80) methods. ChatGPT inter-session similarity ranged from 0.90 to 0.99, compared to 0.34 to 0.98 among human experts, and precaution selection disagreement was only 7.04% across 71 risks.
pdfFile: Oral, M. etal - 2025 - Usability of large language models for building construction safety risk assessment.pdf
